headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 with invisible capability safeguards dominates the week.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model spanning software engineering, vision, analytics, and cybersecurity, with safety systems that restrict high-risk domains such as biology and chemistry. The sharpest detail is a set of silent interventions that degrade performance on frontier LLM development requests via prompt modification and steering vectors, targeting roughly 0.03% of traffic rather than refusing outright. Accountability pressure is building in parallel: lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated fake citations, and a German court ruled Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews. On the technique side, real-time inference, attribution, and filter propagation are breaking out, alongside new entrants in scaling laws, auto-scaling, and CPU offloading.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
